codeReview.summary: Well-structured implementation of POST /api/memos/{id}/reprocess that correctly creates new transcription jobs while protecting user edits. The code follows existing patterns (retry_memo, service/API layer separation) and includes thorough test coverage (24 tests across service and API layers).

issues.description: Minor code duplication with retry_memo: the reprocess_memo function duplicates the pattern of fetching the latest job, checking active states, extracting settings, creating a new job, and updating memo status. The logic is nearly identical except reprocess allows completed jobs and resets transcript_revision. This is acceptable for now but could be refactored into a shared helper if more variants are added.

The reprocess endpoint checks memo existence and transcript_revision in the API layer (lines 229-243), then checks active job status (lines 247-255), then calls the service function which re-checks active job status (jobs.py:454). This double-check of active states is harmless but slightly redundant — the API layer could delegate all business logic to the service function. The current approach is consistent with how retry_memo works, so this is a pattern-following choice rather than a bug.
Duplicate `assert new_job is not None` on lines 357-358 and similar on lines 374-375. Harmless but slightly noisy — the first assert already guarantees non-None for the second.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md already documents the invariant 'Reprocessing never silently overwrites user edits' which was added by this feature. This is good — the worker correctly updated the shared knowledge base.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md Key Invariants section includes 'Reprocessing never silently overwrites user edits'

sharedStateObservations.observation: There's an emerging pattern of memo-action endpoints (retry, reprocess, cancel) that follow a consistent structure: check memo exists → check job status guard → delegate to service function → return job response. This pattern is implicit but consistent across jobs.py endpoints. AGENTS.md doesn't document this API endpoint pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/app/api/jobs.py — retry_memo (line 173) and reprocess_memo (line 216) follow identical structure

summary: PASS. The implementation correctly implements POST /api/memos/{id}/reprocess with edit protection via transcript_revision check and confirm parameter. The code follows existing project patterns, the TDD approach was properly followed (tests written first, then implementation), and 24 tests provide comprehensive coverage of service and API layers including edge cases (active jobs, missing memos, edited transcripts, confirm parameter variants). Minor non-blocking issues: some code duplication with retry_memo and redundant active-state checking between API and service layers, both consistent with existing patterns. The worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure correctly.
